title: Automate merged Work Item lifecycle closure
outOfScope: automatic deletion of developer-local branches from GitHub-hosted runners

sources.reason: Defines merged-PR lifecycle closure and branch cleanup requirements.
Defines the current manual ai-close-work-item workflow and fail-closed semantics.
Current implementation requires a local Work Item branch and local base worktree.
Existing GitHub Actions event, permission, and repository workflow conventions; this is a template-repository adapter example, not an adopter-wide assumption.
Canonical lifecycle protocol that currently requires manual invocation.
Fixed governance complexity bounds must include the next Work Item's tracked design artifact before remote checks run.

problemStatement: The repository documents lifecycle closure after PR merge but does not automatically invoke or coordinate it. Remote branch cleanup can run in a provider-specific hosted CI/CD adapter, while developer-local branch cleanup cannot be performed by a hosted runner and needs a local trigger or explicit handoff. The template must not hard-code GitHub Actions into adopter projects because adopters may use GitLab or another provider.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Merged PR triggers safe remote cleanup only after merge and required evidence checks
scenarioCoverage.reason: Requires a design decision and implementation in a follow-up coding phase.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Developer-local branch is cleaned after the remote merge without deleting an unmerged branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: A hosted CI/CD runner cannot directly mutate a developer's local clone; the local trigger remains to be designed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Cleanup races with provider-side automatic branch deletion
evidence: PR #41 follow-up context and the idempotent deletion behavior in scripts/ai_close_work_item.py

unknowns: Whether the target automation should be hosted-provider-only, local post-merge only, or a coordinated two-part workflow.
How archived Contract/Summary evidence and the merged request identity should be passed to a hosted cleanup job without checking out the deleted source branch.
Which provider credentials and protected-branch conditions are acceptable for automatic remote branch deletion.
Whether local cleanup may be triggered by an explicit developer pull/sync command or must be fully transparent after merge.
Which provider-neutral evidence sink should receive failed or pending CI/CD results: request note, Work Item Summary, status/check annotation, or a combination.

riskAssessment.reason: Automation would be authorized to delete remote branches and could incorrectly remove recovery paths if merge identity, evidence, permissions, or event timing are wrong.

agentCapability.blockedReason: Initial skeleton; clear unknowns and confirm verification before coding.

executionDecision.reason: Initial skeleton must be completed before execution.

preReviewWarnings: Do not claim full lifecycle automation unless the local developer branch boundary is explicitly handled.
Review provider credential permissions, merge-only gating, idempotency, and recovery behavior before coding.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The investigation documents the current manual gap and distinguishes hosted remote cleanup from developer-local branch cleanup.
Two or more viable automation approaches are compared for trigger, permissions, evidence transport, race handling, recovery, and local-clone limitations.

The design defines provider-neutral CI/CD monitoring keyed by merged request head SHA, required-check aggregation, pending/failed blocking, and failure evidence retention, with GitHub Actions documented only as this template's adapter example.
The design explicitly prevents adopter installation from assuming GitHub Actions and defines GitLab or other providers as adapter-specific or manual fail-closed paths.
The governance complexity policy is updated before implementation to the fixed tracked-file bound required by the PR41-merged main baseline plus this Work Item's tracked design artifact.
A recommended design and explicit non-goals are recorded in the design document before any production workflow or cleanup behavior is changed.
The Work Item records the remaining decisions required to enter a separate coding phase.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: After PR merge, the repository still depends on a human to invoke lifecycle closure, and the current rules do not explain how remote automation relates to local branch cleanup.
constraints: Do not mix this investigation with PR #41.
Never delete a branch unless merge identity and final state are verified.
Do not imply that a hosted runner can delete a developer's local branch.
Keep the existing fail-closed lifecycle command as the safety reference.
intent.rationale: A separate evidence-backed design is needed before granting automation destructive permissions or changing the lifecycle contract.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly requested the fixed governance threshold be updated before finishing the current Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
